tsTroubleshooting.domains: (topic tsTroubleshooting) (topic hi-d) (topic sdlVariables-c) (topic ut-d) (topic indexing-d) (topic sw-d) (topic ui-d) (topic oce-d) (topic oceCommonElements-c) (topic oceTopic-c hi-d oceHighlight-c) (topic oceTopic-c ui-d oceUi-c) (topic oceTopic-c sw-d oceSoftware-c) (topic oceTopic-c) (topic oceTopic-c task oceTask-c) (topic oceTopic-c tsTroubleshooting oceTroubleshooting-c)
tsTroubleshooting.title: 11 System administrator password lost

abstract.p: The System administrator password is lost.
Three options are possible in case the System administrator password is lost:

li.p: System administrator password is lost and e-mail server and account are configured
System administrator password is lost and no e-mail server and account is configured
System administrator password is lost and no e-mail server and account is configured and no backup is present

tsCauses.p: Option 1: System administrator password is lost and e-mail server and account are configured

tsResolve.p: In case the password is lost, it can be reset by the system administrator himself. No service intervention required.

This procedure is performed by means of an e-mail. At the installation of the system an e-mail server and an mail account in the Settings Editor (Configuration > Mail server > Edit) was done. For detailed information: See Operation and Safety information Operation and Safety information.

tsCauses.p: Option 2: System administrator password is lost and no e-mail server and account is configured

tsResolve.p: Use a backup, that was created at the moment that the administrator password was still known.

tsCauses.p: Option 3: System administrator password is lost and no e-mail server and account is configured and no backup is present

tsResolve.p: Re-install the system software with ORS installation or USB key installation created with keepsettings=No.

note.p: Do not restore a backup and reconfigure the settings manually.
